task: I need to send a vendor a short message explaining that I’m not accepting their revised terms. The situation is already tense, and I want the note to stay brief and professional so it doesn’t create extra back-and-forth.

turns.agent_response:
Keep it short, factual, and final.

**Template:**

> Thanks for sending the revised terms. We’re not able to accept them as proposed, so we’ll be moving in a different direction. Please send over any final invoice or closeout items, and we’ll take it from there.

If you want it a bit firmer:

> Thanks for the revision. We’re not accepting the updated terms. Please provide any final billing or closeout documentation, and we’ll handle next steps from there.

If you want, I can make it **softer** or **more direct** depending on how much relationship you want to preserve.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I want it a little tighter and less chatty. No “thanks,” and don’t invite a back-and-forth. Just a clean rejection and next step. Something like:

We’re not accepting the revised terms. Please send the final invoice and any closeout documentation.

Or a slightly firmer version:

We’re not able to accept the revised terms. Please forward the final invoice and any closeout items so we can close this out.

If you want, give me 2-3 versions: neutral, firmer, and more legal.

turns.agent_response:
Here are three tight versions:

**Neutral**
> We’re not accepting the revised terms. Please send the final invoice and any closeout documentation.

**Firmer**
> We’re not able to accept the revised terms. Please forward the final invoice and any closeout items so we can close this out.

**More legal**
> We do not accept the revised terms. Please send the final invoice and all closeout documentation so the matter can be concluded.

If you want, I can make them **colder** or **more relationship-preserving**.

user_simulator.text: Neutral is fine. “We’re not accepting the revised terms. Please send the final invoice and any closeout documentation.” Clean enough. No extra language needed.
